title: Storage driver set in /etc/containers/storage.conf ignored for rootless configurations
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Podman does not use the storage driver configured in `/etc/containers/storage.conf` as the default storage driver for rootless containers.
When I set the driver as `btrfs` in `/etc/containers/storage.conf`, then reset rootless and delete any user configuration, it uses `vfs` instead.
This behavior is not mentioned in the documentation [here](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/docs/tutorials/rootless_tutorial.md#storageconf), so I assume this is a bug.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Install Podman from the Kubic repository on Ubuntu 18.04.

2. Set the driver as `btrfs` in `/etc/containers/storage.conf`.

3. Run `podman info` as a user and check the value for `graphDriverName` only to find it is `vfs`.

**Describe the results you received:**

The `graphDriverName` for the user's rootless configuration is `vfs`.

**Describe the results you expected:**

The `graphDriverName` for the user's rootless configuration should be `btrfs`.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version
Version:      3.0.1
API Version:  3.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.2
Built:        Wed Dec 31 18:00:00 1969
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

```

```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Physical machine using Btrfs on root.

comments.body:
I would like to know if this is working properly in rootfull mode?

I don't think we currently support btrfs in rootless mode, but If it works, I guess we should allow it.  The biggest issue is no one has used it in the past, so we have no idea how well it works.
I am also no quite sure if this is allowed in rootless mode.   @jwillikers interested in opening a PR to see if you could get this to work?

Yes, this works fine for the root configuration in my case.

```sh
$ sudo podman info | grep graphDriverName
  graphDriverName: btrfs
```

I've already verified that the `btrfs` driver works correctly in rootless mode, but I do have to configure the storage driver explicitly in `~/.config/containers/storage.conf`.
It creates Btrfs subvolumes under `/home/jordan/.local/share/containers/storage/btrfs/subvolumes/`.
I even verified from within a running container that the Btrfs mount options used for it were properly inherited from the parent subvolume.

I can certainly do a PR.
Since the driver appears to work fine from rootless, would the PR be for enabling rootless configurations to inherit the Btrfs driver from the `/etc/containers/storage.conf` file?
Or is some other work, needed?

Yes I would say that if BTRFS is set in /etc/containers/storage.conf then the rootless container storage should follow it.

Similarly if I put driver=btrfs in ~/.config/containers/storage.conf then rootless mode should use it.
Got it. 👍 I think I've found the related logic in [containers/storage/types/options.go](https://github.com/containers/storage/blob/master/types/options.go#L166).
Yes the changes would be in containers/storage.
